comment: Same label and intended meaning (people attending/participating).
Both represent documents (same lexical label and role).
Review concept has equivalent use in both ontologies (documents evaluating papers).
Session classes correspond in conference modelling (same label, similar restrictions).
Individual presentation (EKAW) and Presentation (IASTED) align closely (both are conference presentations); EKAW emphasises disjointness with Workshop. Structural restrictions differ slightly.
Paper_Author in EKAW and Author in IASTED play similar roles (person producing manuscripts/presentations). Labels differ slightly but semantics align.
EKAW proceedings (volumes containing accepted papers) and IASTED Book_proceeding are similar publishing concepts; modelling differences exist so marked as closeMatch.
Property connecting Document->Person (author) in EKAW corresponds to write in IASTED (inverse is is_writen_by). Labels and domain/range align closely.
authorOf (EKAW) is inverse of writtenBy; maps to IASTED write property (same authoring relation).
EKAW hasReview links Paper->Review; IASTED uses is_writen_by for Review->Reviewer (different orientation). They are related but not equivalent (inverse/role differences).
